title: poa pratensis subsp. costata
intro: Poa pratensis subsp. costata, also known as the mountain meadow grass, is a subspecies of the Kentucky bluegrass that is native to mountainous regions of the world, particularly in Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Poa pratensis subsp. costata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to make infusions and decoctions.
